description: Fuel evaporation and spillage. Base rate is 6% per game-turn for all fuel on the map (not in convoys at sea), rounded down. Commonwealth rate is 9% from Sept 1940 through the last game-turn of August 1941 (inclusive). Hot weather adds +5% taken immediately when hot weather is determined.

cases.description: Axis fuel dump, any date, no hot weather: base 6% applies
cases.rule_clause: each Player reduces all fuel levels on the game-map (not in convoys at sea) by six percent (6%), rounded down

cases.description: Commonwealth fuel dump at campaign start (1940-09-09): 9% rate applies (Sept 1940 is the start of the elevated CW rate)
cases.rule_clause: from Sept., 1940 until the last Game-Turn (inclusive) in August, 1941, the Commonwealth spillage and evaporation rate is nine percent (9%) per Game-turn

cases.description: Commonwealth fuel dump on last day of August 1941 (1941-08-31): still 9% (last game-turn inclusive)
cases.rule_clause: from Sept., 1940 until the last Game-Turn (inclusive) in August, 1941, the Commonwealth spillage and evaporation rate is nine percent (9%) per Game-turn

cases.description: Commonwealth fuel dump on 1941-09-01 (first day after cutoff): reverts to base 6%
cases.rule_clause: from Sept., 1940 until the last Game-Turn (inclusive) in August, 1941, the Commonwealth spillage and evaporation rate is nine percent (9%) per Game-turn

cases.description: Commonwealth fuel dump in 1942: 6% base rate applies (well past the Aug 1941 cutoff)
cases.rule_clause: from Sept., 1940 until the last Game-Turn (inclusive) in August, 1941, the Commonwealth spillage and evaporation rate is nine percent (9%) per Game-turn

cases.description: Axis fuel dump with hot weather: 6% base + 5% hot weather = 11% total
cases.rule_clause: if the weather for an operations stage is 'hot weather', an additional reduction of five per-cent (5%) is taken as soon as the hot weather is determined
cases.note: The rule states the hot weather 5% is taken 'as soon as the hot weather is determined', which is separate from the base 6% in the Stores Expenditure Stage; the fixture combines them into a single net rate for simplicity.

cases.description: Commonwealth fuel dump in early period (1940-09 to 1941-08) with hot weather: 9% + 5% = 14% total
cases.rule_clause: from Sept., 1940 until the last Game-Turn (inclusive) in August, 1941, the Commonwealth spillage and evaporation rate is nine percent (9%) per Game-turn [plus] an additional reduction of five per-cent (5%) [for hot weather]

cases.description: Fuel level of zero: no loss regardless of rate
cases.rule_clause: each Player reduces all fuel levels on the game-map (not in convoys at sea) by six percent (6%), rounded down

cases.description: Non-integer result at 6% verifies floor (not round) rounding: 333 * 0.06 = 19.98 → floor = 19
cases.rule_clause: each Player reduces all fuel levels on the game-map (not in convoys at sea) by six percent (6%), rounded down

cases.description: Non-integer result at 9% verifies floor rounding: 333 * 0.09 = 29.97 → floor = 29
cases.rule_clause: the Commonwealth spillage and evaporation rate is nine percent (9%) per Game-turn

cases.description: Non-integer result at 11% verifies floor rounding: 333 * 0.11 = 36.63 → floor = 36
cases.rule_clause: each Player reduces all fuel levels on the game-map (not in convoys at sea) by six percent (6%), rounded down [plus] an additional reduction of five per-cent (5%)

cases.description: Fuel in convoys at sea is explicitly exempt from evaporation; lost = 0
cases.rule_clause: each Player reduces all fuel levels on the game-map (not in convoys at sea) by six percent (6%), rounded down
cases.note: The rule explicitly excludes fuel 'in convoys at sea' from the evaporation reduction. This is stated directly in the rule text, not an implementation detail.
